questions.question: Which personality identified as Sugriva in Lord Caitanya's pastimes appears in the verses preceding the description of Ramacandra Puri?
questions.explanation: The previous verse (91) identifies Govindananda as the previous incarnation of Sugriva.

93.verse_text: Lord Caitanya Himself did not accept that Ramacandra Puri was the incarnation of Vibhisana, but the Lord himself said that Ramacandra Puri was the empowered incarnation of Srimati Radharani's mother-in-law Jatila. This Ramacandra Puri restricted Lord Caitanya in His acceptance of alms. Rcika Muni's son Brahma Mahatapah, and Prahlada Maharaja combined to appear as Haridasa Thakur in Lord Caitanya's pastimes.

questions.question: Which personality is identified as the empowered incarnation of Srimati Radharani's mother-in-law, Jatila, in Lord Caitanya's pastimes?
questions.explanation: The text explicitly states that Lord Caitanya identified Ramacandra Puri as the empowered incarnation of Jatila, Srimati Radharani's mother-in-law.

questions.question: Who combined to appear as Haridasa Thakur in Lord Caitanya's pastimes?
options: Rcika Muni's son Brahma Mahatapah and Prahlada Maharaja
questions.correct_answer: Rcika Muni's son Brahma Mahatapah and Prahlada Maharaja
questions.explanation: According to the verse, Haridasa Thakur is the combined incarnation of Rcika Muni's son, Brahma Mahatapah, and Prahlada Maharaja.

questions.question: Regarding the identity of Ramacandra Puri, what was the stance of Lord Caitanya?
options: He denied he was Vibhisana and identified him as Jatila
He denied he was Jatila and identified him as Vibhisana
questions.correct_answer: He denied he was Vibhisana and identified him as Jatila
questions.explanation: The text states that Lord Caitanya Himself did not accept Ramacandra Puri as the incarnation of Vibhisana, but instead identified him as the empowered incarnation of Jatila.

questions.question: What specific action did Ramacandra Puri take towards Lord Caitanya in his pastimes?
options: He restricted Lord Caitanya's acceptance of alms
He refused to offer alms to the Lord
He blessed the Lord with an abundance of food
questions.correct_answer: He restricted Lord Caitanya's acceptance of alms
questions.explanation: The text explicitly mentions that Ramacandra Puri restricted Lord Caitanya in His acceptance of alms.

94/95.verse_text: In the book Caitanya-carita, Murari Gupta explains that one morning Mahatapah gave an unwashed tulasi leaf to his father, who became so angry at him for this that he cursed him. Because of this Mahatapah took birth in a yavana family as the great devotee Haridasa Thakura.

questions.question: What event caused the devotee known as Mahatapah to take birth in a yavana family as Haridasa Thakura?
options: He offended a brahmana during a sacrifice
He gave his father an unwashed tulasi leaf
He broke a vow of silence
He committed an offense against the deity
questions.correct_answer: He gave his father an unwashed tulasi leaf
questions.explanation: According to the text, Mahatapah's father became angry and cursed him after Mahatapah offered him an unwashed tulasi leaf, leading to his birth as Haridasa Thakura.

questions.question: Which historical devotee of Lord Caitanya's pastimes is identified as the reincarnation of the sage Mahatapah?
questions.explanation: The text explicitly states that because of the curse he received from his father, Mahatapah took birth in a yavana family as the great devotee Haridasa Thakura.

questions.question: In which literary work did Murari Gupta explain the origin of Haridasa Thakura's birth?
questions.explanation: The verse explicitly cites the Caitanya-carita as the book where Murari Gupta explains the circumstances of Mahatapah's birth as Haridasa Thakura.

questions.question: Who cursed Mahatapah, eventually resulting in his birth as Haridasa Thakura?
questions.explanation: The text states that Mahatapah gave an unwashed tulasi leaf to his father, who became angry and cursed him, leading to his birth as Haridasa Thakura.

96/97.verse_text: The eight mystic powers, beginning with anima-siddhi, which had formerly appeared in Vrndavana, took birth in Bengal during Lord Caitanya's pastimes as the eight devotees Ananta, Sukhananda, Govinda, Raghunatha, Krsnananda, Kesava, Damodara, and Raghava. All these devotees bore the title Puri after their names.

questions.question: Which specific group of entities appeared in Bengal as the eight devotees bearing the title 'Puri'?
options: The eight mystic powers beginning with anima-siddhi
The associates who served Krishna in Dvaraka
The sages who spoke the Bhagavata-samhita to Janaka Rsi
questions.correct_answer: The eight mystic powers beginning with anima-siddhi
questions.explanation: The text explicitly states that the eight mystic powers, beginning with anima-siddhi, took birth in Bengal during Lord Caitanya's pastimes as the eight devotees named Ananta, Sukhananda, Govinda, Raghunatha, Krsnananda, Kesava, Damodara, and Raghava, who all bore the title Puri.

questions.question: Which of the following devotees is identified as one of the eight manifestations of the mystic powers in Lord Caitanya's pastimes?
questions.explanation: Raghava is listed as one of the eight devotees who bore the title Puri, representing the eight mystic powers that appeared in Bengal during Lord Caitanya's pastimes.

questions.question: What title was shared by all eight devotees who manifested from the eight mystic powers during Lord Caitanya's pastimes?
questions.explanation: The text explicitly states, 'All these devotees bore the title Puri after their names,' referring to the eight devotees representing the eight mystic powers.

98-101.verse_text: The nine Jayanteyas, who were fixed in celibacy and equal vision, and who spoke the Sri Bhagavata-samhita to Janaka Rsi, appeared on this earth as nine sannyasis, and continually performed pastimes with Lord Caitanya. Their names were: Sri Nrsimhananda Tirtha, Sri Satyananda Bharati, Sri Nrsimha Tirtha, Sri Cidananda Tirtha, Sri Jagannatha Tirtha, Sri Vasudeva Tirtha, Sri Rama Tirtha, Sri Purusottama Tirtha, and Sri Garuda Avadhuta, who was also known as Sri Gopendra Asrama.

questions.question: Which group of transcendental personalities appeared as the nine sannyasis who accompanied Lord Caitanya?
questions.explanation: Verses 98-101 identify that the nine Jayanteyas, known for their celibacy and equal vision, appeared on earth as nine sannyasis to participate in Lord Caitanya's pastimes.

questions.question: Which of the following figures was also known as Sri Gopendra Asrama?
questions.explanation: Verse 101 explicitly states that Sri Garuda Avadhuta was also known as Sri Gopendra Asrama.

questions.question: What activity were the nine Jayanteyas famous for performing before appearing in Lord Caitanya's pastimes?
options: Serving as the guardians of Kuvera's treasury
Speaking the Sri Bhagavata-samhita to Janaka Rsi
Practicing intense meditation in the Himalayan mountains
questions.correct_answer: Speaking the Sri Bhagavata-samhita to Janaka Rsi
questions.explanation: According to verse 98, the nine Jayanteyas were those who spoke the Sri Bhagavata-samhita to Janaka Rsi.

questions.question: How many sannyasis are identified as the incarnations of the Jayanteyas in the pastimes of Lord Caitanya?
questions.explanation: The text states in verse 98 that the nine Jayanteyas appeared on this earth as nine sannyasis.

102/103.verse_text: Kuvera's famous nine treasures, which include his conch and lotus, took birth during Lord Caitanya's pastimes as the nine sons of Nidhiratna-devi. Their names were Srinidhi, Srigarbha, Kaviratna, Sudhanidhi, Vidyanidhi, Gunanidhi, the learned brahmana Ratnabahu, Sriman Acaryaratna, and Sri Ratnakara Pandita.

questions.question: Which divine treasures manifested as the nine sons of Nidhiratna-devi during the pastimes of Lord Caitanya?
options: The nine incarnations of Garga Muni
The nine sannyasi associates of Lord Caitanya
questions.explanation: The text states that Kuvera's famous nine treasures, including his conch and lotus, took birth during Lord Caitanya's pastimes as the nine sons of Nidhiratna-devi.

questions.question: Which of the following was one of the nine sons of Nidhiratna-devi?
questions.explanation: According to the verse, Sri Ratnakara Pandita is listed as one of the nine sons of Nidhiratna-devi, who were the incarnations of Kuvera's nine treasures.

questions.question: How many sons did Nidhiratna-devi have during the pastimes of Lord Caitanya?
questions.explanation: The text identifies that Kuvera's nine treasures took birth as the nine sons of Nidhiratna-devi.

questions.question: Who is identified as the mother of the incarnations of Kuvera's nine treasures?
questions.explanation: The text states that the nine treasures of Kuvera took birth as the nine sons of Nidhiratna-devi.

104.verse_text: Before the assembled relatives and friends, Nilambara Cakravarti spoke the astrological prediction of Lord Caitanya's future life. For this reason it is said the Nilambara Cakravarti is the incarnation of Garga Muni.

questions.question: Which personality from Krishna-lila is identified as the incarnation of Nilambara Cakravarti in Lord Caitanya's pastimes?
questions.explanation: The text explicitly states that Nilambara Cakravarti is the incarnation of Garga Muni, based on his role in speaking the astrological prediction of Lord Caitanya's future.

questions.question: What event served as the basis for the identification of Nilambara Cakravarti as Garga Muni?
options: The birth of the nine sons of Nidhiratna-devi
The migration of Saci-devi's parents from Vrajabhumi
The act of speaking the astrological prediction of Lord Caitanya's future life
questions.correct_answer: The act of speaking the astrological prediction of Lord Caitanya's future life
questions.explanation: The text explains that because Nilambara Cakravarti spoke the astrological prediction of Lord Caitanya's future life before friends and relatives, he is considered the incarnation of Garga Muni.

105.verse_text: Srimati Saci-devi's father had formerly been the cowherd Sumukha, who was Yasoda-devi's father. Saci-devi's mother had formerly been Patala-devi, the wife of Sumukha in Vrajabhumi.

questions.question: Which personality from Vrajabhumi is identified as the former incarnation of Saci-devi's father?
questions.explanation: Verse 105 states that Srimati Saci-devi's father was formerly the cowherd Sumukha, who was the father of Yasoda-devi.

questions.question: Who is identified as the previous incarnation of Saci-devi's mother?
questions.explanation: According to verse 105, Saci-devi's mother was formerly Patala-devi, the wife of Sumukha in Vrajabhumi.

questions.question: In the pastimes of Vrajabhumi, what was the relationship between Patala-devi and the cowherd Sumukha?
questions.explanation: Verse 105 identifies Patala-devi as the wife of Sumukha in Vrajabhumi.

questions.question: Which personality in Vrajabhumi was the daughter of the cowherd Sumukha?
questions.explanation: Verse 105 specifies that the cowherd Sumukha was the father of Yasoda-devi.

106.verse_text: Lord Caitanya's associate Sri Devananda Pandita, who was very learned in the Puranas, had previously been Bhaguri Muni, the chief pandita in the court of Maharaja Nanda.

questions.question: Which personality from the court of Maharaja Nanda was incarnated as the associate of Lord Caitanya known as Devananda Pandita?
questions.explanation: Verse 106 explicitly states that Devananda Pandita was previously Bhaguri Muni, who served as the chief pandita in the court of Maharaja Nanda.

questions.question: In his previous incarnation, what role did the associate of Lord Caitanya known as Devananda Pandita hold in Vrajabhumi?
options: Chief pandita in the court of Maharaja Nanda
A cowherd friend of Nanda Maharaja
An advisor to the four Kumaras
questions.correct_answer: Chief pandita in the court of Maharaja Nanda
questions.explanation: The text identifies Devananda Pandita as having been Bhaguri Muni, who was the chief pandita in Maharaja Nanda's court.

questions.question: What primary qualification or field of knowledge is Devananda Pandita noted for in the text?
options: Expertise in the four Kumaras' teachings
Expertise in the genealogy of Vrajabhumi
Expertise in the court rituals of Maharaja Nanda
questions.explanation: Verse 106 describes Devananda Pandita as being very learned in the Puranas.

questions.question: Who was the previous identity of the devotee known as Devananda Pandita?
questions.explanation: According to verse 106, Sri Devananda Pandita was previously Bhaguri Muni.

107.verse_text: The four learned devotees in Lord Caitanya's pastimes, named Kasinatha, Lokanatha, Srinatha, and Ramanatha, were incarnations of the four Kumaras. Of this there is no doubt.

questions.question: Which four devotees in Lord Caitanya's pastimes are identified as the incarnations of the four Kumaras?
questions.explanation: Verse 107 explicitly states that the four learned devotees named Kasinatha, Lokanatha, Srinatha, and Ramanatha were incarnations of the four Kumaras.

questions.question: Who did the four devotees known as the 'four Nathas' incarnate as in their previous divine identity?
options: The four sages of the court of Nanda
The four primary associates of Advaita Acarya
questions.explanation: The text identifies the four devotees (Kasinatha, Lokanatha, Srinatha, and Ramanatha) as incarnations of the four Kumaras.

questions.question: Which of the following associates of Lord Caitanya was previously Bhaguri Muni?
questions.explanation: The previous verse establishes that Devananda Pandita, a scholar of the Puranas, was previously Bhaguri Muni, the chief pandita in the court of Maharaja Nanda.

108.verse_text: The names of these four devotees each end with the word 'natha', just as the names of the four Kumaras each end with the word 'kumara'. These four devotees became known as the 'four Nathas'.

questions.question: Which four devotees in Lord Caitanya's pastimes are considered the incarnations of the four Kumaras?
options: Vrndavana das Thakura, Kusumapida, Kasinatha, and Ramanatha
questions.explanation: The previous verse explicitly identifies Kasinatha, Lokanatha, Srinatha, and Ramanatha as the incarnations of the four Kumaras.

questions.question: What collective title is given to Kasinatha, Lokanatha, Srinatha, and Ramanatha?
questions.explanation: Verse 108 states that because their names end with the word 'natha', these four devotees became known as the 'four Nathas'.

questions.question: The names of Kasinatha, Lokanatha, Srinatha, and Ramanatha are compared to the names of which group due to their shared suffix?
questions.explanation: Verse 108 explains that the 'four Nathas' share a naming convention similar to the four Kumaras, whose names all end with 'kumara'.

questions.question: Who appeared as Vrndavana das Thakura in Lord Caitanya's pastimes?
questions.explanation: The next verse states that Vedavyasa appeared in Lord Caitanya's pastimes as Vrndavana das Thakura.

questions.question: Besides Vedavyasa, what other personality entered the body of Vrndavana das Thakura?
questions.explanation: The next verse specifies that the cowherd friend of Lord Krsna, Kusumapida, also entered the body of Vrndavana das Thakura.

109.verse_text: Vedavyasa appeared in Lord Caitanya's pastimes as Vrndavana das Thakura. Lord Krsna's cowherd friend Kusumapida also entered the body of Vrndavana das Thakura.

questions.question: Which personality from Krishna's pastimes entered the body of Vrndavana das Thakura alongside Vedavyasa?
questions.explanation: Verse 109 explicitly states that the cowherd friend of Lord Krsna, Kusumapida, entered the body of Vrndavana das Thakura along with the appearance of Vedavyasa.

questions.question: Who is identified as the personality of Vedavyasa appearing within the pastimes of Lord Caitanya?
questions.explanation: According to verse 109, Vedavyasa appeared in the pastimes of Lord Caitanya as Vrndavana das Thakura.

questions.question: What is the relationship between Vedavana das Thakura and Kusumapida according to the text?
options: They are the same person as Vallabha Bhatta.
They both entered the body of the same personality.
They are described as the 'four Nathas'.
They are separate associates who never interacted.
questions.correct_answer: They both entered the body of the same personality.
questions.explanation: Verse 109 explains that both Vedavyasa and the cowherd friend Kusumapida entered the body of Vrndavana das Thakura.

110.verse_text: Sukadeva Gosvami, the son of Srila Vyasadeva, appeared during Lord Caitanya's pastimes as the devotee named Vallabha Bhatta.

questions.question: Which personality from the pastimes of Lord Caitanya is identified as the incarnation of Sukadeva Gosvami?
questions.explanation: Verse 110 states that Sukadeva Gosvami, the son of Srila Vyasadeva, appeared during Lord Caitanya's pastimes as the devotee named Vallabha Bhatta.

questions.question: Who was the father of Sukadeva Gosvami in his eternal identity?
questions.explanation: The text identifies Sukadeva Gosvami specifically as the son of Srila Vyasadeva.

questions.question: In the pastimes of Lord Caitanya, Vallabha Bhatta is recognized as the manifestation of which divine figure?
questions.explanation: The text explicitly states that Sukadeva Gosvami appeared as Vallabha Bhatta during the pastimes of Lord Caitanya.

111.verse_text: Sri Jagannatha Acarya, who was also known as Gangadasa Pandita, was very dear to Lord Caitanya. In his previous life he had been Durvasa Muni, who delighted the gopis and was very dear to them.

questions.question: Which personality from the pastimes of Lord Caitanya is identified as the previous incarnation of Durvasa Muni?
questions.explanation: Verse 111 identifies Sri Jagannatha Acarya, also known as Gangadasa Pandita, as the previous incarnation of Durvasa Muni.

questions.question: By what other name was Jagannatha Acarya known?
questions.explanation: Verse 111 explicitly states that Sri Jagannatha Acarya was also known as Gangadasa Pandita.

questions.question: Who is described as having delighted the gopis and being very dear to them in a previous life?
questions.explanation: Verse 111 states that Durvasa Muni was very dear to the gopis and delighted them in his previous life.

questions.question: In Lord Caitanya's pastimes, who appeared as the devotee named Vallabha Bhatta?
questions.explanation: The previous verse (preceding 111) identifies Sukadeva Gosvami as appearing in Lord Caitanya's pastimes as Vallabha Bhatta.

112.verse_text:
The learned devotees know that Candrasekhara Acarya was an incarnation of the moon-god and Sriman Uddhava dasa was an empowered incarnation of the moon-god.
113. Lord Caitanya personally affirmed that Candrasekhara Acarya was an incarnation of the moon- god, and in the same way the Lord expressly said that Sriman Visvesvara Acarya was an incarnation of the sun-god.

questions.question: Which personality is identified as an incarnation of the moon-god in the pastimes of Lord Caitanya?
questions.explanation: Verse 112 explicitly states that the learned devotees know Candrasekhara Acarya was an incarnation of the moon-god.

questions.question: Who is identified as an empowered incarnation of the moon-god?
questions.explanation: According to verse 112, Sriman Uddhava dasa is recognized as an empowered incarnation of the moon-god.

questions.question: Whose incarnation as the sun-god was expressly affirmed by Lord Caitanya?
questions.explanation: Verse 113 states that Lord Caitanya expressly said that Sriman Visvesvara Acarya was an incarnation of the sun-god.

questions.question: Which two personalities are identified as incarnations of the moon-god or an empowered incarnation of the moon-god?
options: Candrasekhara Acarya and Sriman Uddhava dasa
Candrasekhara Acarya and Sriman Visvesvara Acarya
Sriman Uddhava dasa and Bhaskara Thakura
Sriman Visvesvara Acarya and Candrasekhara Acarya
questions.correct_answer: Candrasekhara Acarya and Sriman Uddhava dasa
questions.explanation: Verse 112 identifies Candrasekhara Acarya as an incarnation of the moon-god and Sriman Uddhava dasa as an empowered incarnation of the moon-god.

114.verse_text:
Bhaskara Thakura was an incarnation of the demigod Visvakarma. Vanamali Tahkura was an incarnation of Sudama Brahmana, who was afflicted by great poverty and obtained great wealth from the Lord.
115. Jaya and Vijaya, the two doorkeepers of Vaikunthaloka, voluntarily appeared in Lord Caitanya's pastimes as the two devotees Sri Jagannatha and Sri Madhava.

questions.question: Which demigod is Bhaskara Thakura identified as an incarnation of?
questions.explanation: Verse 114 states that Bhaskara Thakura was an incarnation of the demigod Visvakarma.

questions.question: Who is the personality described as being afflicted by great poverty and receiving great wealth from the Lord, appearing in Lord Caitanya's pastimes as Vanamali Thakura?
questions.explanation: Verse 114 identifies Vanamali Thakura as the incarnation of Sudama Brahmana, who was known for suffering from poverty and being blessed with wealth by the Lord.

questions.question: Which two devotees appeared in Lord Caitanya's pastimes as the doorkeepers of Vaikunthaloka, Jaya and Vijaya?
questions.explanation: Verse 115 explicitly identifies Jaya and Vijaya, the Vaikuntha doorkeepers, as appearing in Lord Caitanya's pastimes as Sri Jagannatha and Sri Madhava.

questions.question: What is the divine role associated with the devotees known as Sri Jagannatha and Sri Madhava?
options: They are incarnations of the moon-god
They are the doorkeepers of Vaikunthaloka
They are the incarnations of Visvakarma
They are the liberated souls Pundarikaksa and Kumuda
questions.correct_answer: They are the doorkeepers of Vaikunthaloka
questions.explanation: Verse 115 states that Jaya and Vijaya, the two doorkeepers of Vaikunthaloka, appeared as the devotees Sri Jagannatha and Sri Madhava.

116.verse_text: The two liberated souls Pundarikaksa and Kumuda, who are very famous in Vaikunthaloka, appeared in Bengal as the two devotees name Govinda and Garuda, who were both very dear to Lord Caitanya Mahaprabhu.

questions.question: Which two famous liberated souls from Vaikunthaloka appeared in Bengal as Govinda and Garuda?
questions.explanation: Verse 116 explicitly states that the two liberated souls Pundarikaksa and Kumuda appeared in Bengal as the devotees Govinda and Garuda.

questions.question: In the pastimes of Lord Caitanya, who were the two devotees known as Govinda and Garuda incarnations of?
options: The demigod Visvakarma and Sudama Brahmana
questions.explanation: According to verse 116, the devotees Govinda and Garuda are the incarnations of the two famous Vaikunthaloka souls, Pundarikaksa and Kumuda.

questions.question: Which of the following describes the relationship between Lord Caitanya and the devotees Govinda and Garuda?
options: They were very dear to Him
They were not associated with Him
questions.correct_answer: They were very dear to Him
questions.explanation: Verse 116 specifies that the two devotees Govinda and Garuda, who are incarnations of Pundarikaksa and Kumuda, were very dear to Lord Caitanya Mahaprabhu.

117.verse_text: Garuda Pandita was the incarnation of Garuda. Gopinatha Simha was the incarnation of Akrura. Some say that Kesava Bharati was the incarnation of Akrura.

questions.question: Which personality is identified in the text as an incarnation of Garuda?
questions.explanation: The verse explicitly states that Garuda Pandita was the incarnation of Garuda.

questions.question: Who is explicitly identified as an incarnation of Akrura in the text?
questions.explanation: The text identifies Gopinatha Simha as the incarnation of Akrura.

questions.question: Which figure is mentioned by some as also being the incarnation of Akrura?
questions.explanation: The text notes that while Gopinatha Simha is the incarnation of Akrura, some also attribute this identity to Kesava Bharati.

questions.question: In the context of the identified incarnations, which personality is paired with the identity of Akrura?
questions.explanation: Gopinatha Simha is stated to be the incarnation of Akrura.

118.verse_text: Sri Paramananda Puri had formerly been Uddhava. Maharaja Prataparudra, who was as powerful as Lord Indra, had formerly been Maharaja Indradyumna, who began the worship of Lord Jagannatha.

questions.question: Which personality is identified as the former incarnation of Sri Paramananda Puri?
questions.explanation: Verse 118 explicitly identifies Sri Paramananda Puri as having formerly been Uddhava.

questions.question: In his previous incarnation, who was Maharaja Prataparudra?
questions.explanation: According to the text, Maharaja Prataparudra had formerly been Maharaja Indradyumna, the one who began the worship of Lord Jagannatha.

questions.question: Which historical figure is described as having begun the worship of Lord Jagannatha in a previous life?
questions.explanation: The text states that Maharaja Prataparudra had formerly been Maharaja Indradyumna, who initiated the worship of Lord Jagannatha.

questions.question: Which individual is compared to Lord Indra due to his power?
questions.explanation: Verse 118 notes that Maharaja Prataparudra was as powerful as Lord Indra.

119.verse_text: Sarvabhauma Bhattacarya had formerly been Brhaspati, the foremost scholar in the heavenly planets.

questions.question: Which divine personality was Sarvabhauma Bhattacarya in his previous identity?
questions.explanation: Verse 119 states that Sarvabhauma Bhattacarya had formerly been Brhaspati, who is described as the foremost scholar in the heavenly planets.

questions.question: Who was known as the foremost scholar in the heavenly planets before appearing as Sarvabhauma Bhattacarya?
questions.explanation: According to the text, Brhaspati was the foremost scholar in the heavenly planets and is identified as the previous incarnation of Sarvabhauma Bhattacarya.

120.verse_text: The cowherd boy Arjuna who was a close friend of the Lord in Vrndavana, and the Pandava Arjuna, both joined to become Ramananda Raya, who was very dear to Lord Caitanya.

questions.question: Which two historical figures combined to become the personality of Ramananda Raya?
options: The cowherd boy Arjuna and the Pandava Arjuna
The cowherd boy Arjuna and Brhaspati
questions.correct_answer: The cowherd boy Arjuna and the Pandava Arjuna
questions.explanation: Verse 120 explicitly states that the cowherd boy Arjuna from Vrndavana and the Pandava Arjuna joined together to manifest as Ramananda Raya.

questions.question: Who is the personality identified as Ramananda Raya in the pastimes of Lord Caitanya?
options: A combination of the cowherd boy Arjuna and the Pandava Arjuna
questions.correct_answer: A combination of the cowherd boy Arjuna and the Pandava Arjuna
questions.explanation: The text identifies Ramananda Raya as the combined identity of the Vrndavana cowherd boy Arjuna and the Pandava Arjuna.

121.verse_text: This exalted personality, Ramanda Raya, for many days described to Lord Caitanya the true nature of pure love for Sri Sri Radha-Krsna.

questions.question: What subject did Ramananda Raya describe to Lord Caitanya over the course of many days?
options: The various incarnations of the Panca-tattva
The true nature of pure love for Sri Sri Radha-Krsna
The pastimes of the Pandavas in the forest
questions.correct_answer: The true nature of pure love for Sri Sri Radha-Krsna
questions.explanation: Verse 121 explicitly states that Ramananda Raya described the true nature of pure love for Sri Sri Radha-Krsna to Lord Caitanya for many days.

questions.question: Which three identities are concluded to be present within the incarnation of Ramananda Raya?
options: Bhavananda Raya, Pandava Arjuna, and Lord Caitanya
Arjuna of Vrndavana, Lalita-gopi, and Maharaja Pandu
Arjuniya-gopi, Maharaja Pandu, and the Pandava Arjuna
questions.explanation: The text states that one may conclude Ramananda Raya is the incarnation of Lalita-gopi, Arjuniya-gopi, and Pandava Arjuna.

questions.question: According to the most learned devotees, who besides Pandava Arjuna is embodied in Ramananda Raya?
questions.explanation: The text notes that the most learned devotees say Ramananda Raya is the incarnation of both the Pandava Arjuna and a gopi named Arjuniya.

questions.question: Which authority is cited to support the conclusion that Ramananda Raya is the incarnation of both Pandava Arjuna and Arjuniya-gopi?
options: The personal diary of Bhavananda Raya
questions.explanation: The text states that the explanation involving the Pandava Arjuna and Arjuniya-gopi is supported by the statements of the Padma Purana, Uttara-khanda.

questions.question: What identity was ascribed to Bhavananda Raya by Lord Caitanya?
questions.explanation: The text mentions that Lord Caitanya directly told Ramananda Raya's father, Bhavananda Raya, that he was the incarnation of Maharaja Pandu.

122-124.verse_text: Some say that Ramananda Raya is the incarnation of Lalita-gopi, and others say that because Lord Caitanya directly told Ramananda Raya's father, Bhavananda Raya, that he was the incarnation of Maharaja Pandu, Arjuna's father, therefore Ramananda Raya must be the incarnation of Pandava Arjuna. The most learned devotees say that Ramananda Raya is the incarnation of both the Pandava Arjuna, and a gopi named Arjuniya. This explanation is also supported by the statements of the Padma Purana, Uttara-khanda. 148-150.verse_text:
Srila Svarupa Damodara Gosvami has also confirmed that the goddess of fortune, who appeared in Vrndavana and was very dear to Lord Krsna, has now appeared as Sri Gadadhara Pandita, who is filled with love for Lord Caitanya Mahaprabhu. Lalita-gopi always follows Srimati Radharani, and therefore she is known as Anuradha (Radha's follower).
When Srimati Radharani appeared as Gadadhara Pandita, Lalita followed her, entering the body of Gadadhara Pandita. Therefore, Gadadhara Pandita is the incarnation of both Srimati Radharani and Lalita-gopi. This is described in the following statement of Caitanya-candrodaya-nataka.

questions.question: Which two transcendental personalities are identified as having entered the body of Gadadhara Pandita?
questions.explanation: The text states that when Srimati Radharani appeared as Gadadhara Pandita, Lalita-gopi followed her by entering the body of Gadadhara Pandita.

questions.question: Why is the gopi Lalita known by the name Anuradha?

Gadadhara is therefore the incarnation of three persons: Srimati Radharani, Lalita-gopi, and Lord Hari."

questions.question: Which specific gopis combined to appear as Sivananda Sena?
questions.explanation: The text states that the two gopis Vira-devi and Duti-devi, who were messengers for the gopis' meetings with Lord Krsna, combined to appear as Sivananda Sena.

questions.question: Who was the mother of Kavi Karnapura in her previous identity as a vraja-gopi?
questions.explanation: The text identifies the vraja-gopi Bindumati as the one who appeared as the mother of the author, Kavi Karnapura.

questions.question: What role did Vira-devi and Duti-devi perform in Vraja-lila?
options: They were personal attendants to Srimati Radharani
They were messengers who arranged the gopis' meetings with Krsna
They were the presiding deities of the forest
They were singers who performed for the Lord

questions.explanation: According to the text, Vira-devi and Duti-devi acted as messengers arranging the gopis' meetings with Lord Krsna.

177.verse_text: Srimati Radharani's dear friend, the vraja-gopi named Madhumati-devi, appeared as Narahari Sarakara, who was very dear to Lord Caitanya.

options: Narahari Sarakara is the expansion of Madhumati-devi
Madhumati-devi is the eternal spiritual identity of Narahari Sarakara

They are unrelated figures in the pastimes

questions.explanation: Verse 177 identifies that the vraja-gopi Madhumati-devi appeared as Narahari Sarakara in the pastimes of Lord Caitanya.

178.verse_text: Srimati Radharani's dear friend, the vraja-gopi named Ratnavali-devi appeared as Gopinatha Acarya, who was famous for his purity.

questions.question: Which vraja-gopi is identified as Gopinatha Acarya in Lord Caitanya's pastimes?
questions.explanation: According to Gaura Ganoddesha Dipika verse 178, the vraja-gopi Ratnavali-devi appeared as Gopinatha Acarya.

questions.question: What is the transcendental identity of Gopinatha Acarya according to the text?
options: A friend of Lord Caitanya's flute
questions.explanation: Verse 178 explicitly states that Gopinatha Acarya appeared as the vraja-gopi named Ratnavali-devi.

questions.question: For what quality was Gopinatha Acarya famous in the pastimes of Lord Caitanya?
questions.explanation: Verse 178 identifies Gopinatha Acarya as being famous for his purity.

179.verse_text: Lord Krsna's favorite flute appeared as Vamsidasa Thakura.

questions.question: Which personality is identified as the appearance of Lord Krsna's favorite flute in the pastimes of Lord Caitanya?
questions.explanation: According to Gaura Ganoddesha Dipika verse 179, Lord Krsna's favorite flute appeared as Vamsidasa Thakura.

questions.question: In the context of the associates of Lord Caitanya, who is revealed to be the incarnation of Lord Krsna's favorite flute?
questions.explanation: The text explicitly states that Lord Krsna's favorite flute appeared as Vamsidasa Thakura.

180.verse_text: Sri Rupa-manjari, who had been famous in Vrndavana-dhama, appeared as Srila Rupa Gosvami.

questions.question: Which transcendental personality appeared in the pastimes of Lord Caitanya as Srila Rupa Gosvami?
questions.explanation: Verse 180 explicitly states that Sri Rupa-manjari, who was famous in Vrndavana-dhama, appeared as Srila Rupa Gosvami.

questions.question: The historical figure Srila Rupa Gosvami is recognized as the appearance of which personality from Vrndavana-dhama?
questions.explanation: According to the text, Sri Rupa-manjari appeared as Srila Rupa Gosvami.

181.verse_text:
Rupa-manjari's closest friend, who was known by the names Rati-manjari and Lavanga-manjari, appeared as Srila Sanatana Gosvami, who was honored by everyone, and who was considered to be like an extension of the transcendental body of Lord Caitanya Mahaprabhu. Sanatana Kumara, the

jewel among the sages, entered the body of Sanatana Gosvami, whi is therefore also considered to be an incarnation of Sanatana Kumara.

questions.question: Which two names are identified as the closest friend of Rupa-manjari?
questions.explanation: Verse 181 explicitly states that Rupa-manjari's closest friend was known by the names Rati-manjari and Lavanga-manjari.

questions.question: Besides being an incarnation of Rati-manjari and Lavanga-manjari, Srila Sanatana Gosvami is also considered an incarnation of which personality?
questions.explanation: Verse 181 explains that the jewel among the sages, Sanatana Kumara, entered the body of Sanatana Gosvami, making him an incarnation of Sanatana Kumara.

questions.question: Who is described as being like an extension of the transcendental body of Lord Caitanya Mahaprabhu?
questions.explanation: Verse 181 identifies Srila Sanatana Gosvami as being considered like an extension of the transcendental body of Lord Caitanya Mahaprabhu.

questions.question: Which personality entered the body of Sanatana Gosvami?
questions.explanation: The text states that Sanatana Kumara, the jewel among the sages, entered the body of Sanatana Gosvami.

182.verse_text: Sivananda Cakravarti, who lived in Vrndavana, is also considered to be an incarnation of Srimati Lavanga-manjari.

questions.question: Which personality is identified as an incarnation of Srimati Lavanga-manjari, alongside their appearance as Srila Sanatana Gosvami?
questions.explanation: Verse 182 explicitly states that Sivananda Cakravarti, who lived in Vrndavana, is also considered an incarnation of Srimati Lavanga-manjari.

questions.question: Which personality is considered the dual incarnation of both Srimati Lavanga-manjari and Sanatana Kumara?
questions.explanation: The text identifies Srila Sanatana Gosvami as an appearance of Lavanga-manjari (who was also known as Rati-manjari) and notes that Sanatana Kumara entered his body, making him an incarnation of Sanatana Kumara.

questions.question: In the context of the associates of Lord Caitanya, who is Sivananda Cakravarti identified as?
questions.explanation: Verse 182 states that Sivananda Cakravarti is considered to be an incarnation of Srimati Lavanga-manjari.

184.verse_text: Ananga-manjari appeared as Srila Gopala Bhatta Gosvami. Some say that Gopala Bhatta Gosvami was actually the incarnation of Sri Guna-manjari.

questions.question: Which personality is identified in the text as the incarnation of Ananga-manjari?
questions.explanation: Verse 184 explicitly states that Ananga-manjari appeared as Srila Gopala Bhatta Gosvami.

questions.question: Besides Ananga-manjari, which other personality is suggested by some to be the incarnation of Srila Gopala Bhatta Gosvami?
questions.explanation: The text notes in verse 184 that while Ananga-manjari appeared as Gopala Bhatta Gosvami, some say he was actually the incarnation of Sri Guna-manjari.

185.verse_text: Raga-manjari appeared as Srila Raghunatha bhatta Gosvami, who lived in a cottage by the shore of Radha-kunda.

questions.question: Which personality appeared as Srila Raghunatha Bhatta Gosvami?
questions.explanation: Verse 185 explicitly states that Raga-manjari appeared as Srila Raghunatha Bhatta Gosvami.

questions.explanation: The text states that Raghunatha Bhatta Gosvami, who is Raga-manjari, lived in a cottage by the shore of Radha-kunda.

questions.question: Who is the eternal identity of Srila Raghunatha Bhatta Gosvami in the spiritual pastimes?
questions.explanation: According to the provided text, Raga-manjari is the personality who appeared as Srila Raghunatha Bhatta Gosvami.

186.verse_text: Srila Raghunatha dasa Gosvami was the incarnation of Rasa-manjari. Some say that Raghunatha das Gosvami was the incarnation of Rati-manjari, and others say he was the incarnation of Bhanumati- devi.

questions.question: Which personality is identified as the incarnation of Rasa-manjari?
questions.explanation: Verse 186 states that Srila Raghunatha dasa Gosvami was the incarnation of Rasa-manjari.

questions.question: Which of the following identities is attributed to Raghunatha dasa Gosvami by some, according to the text?
questions.explanation: Verse 186 mentions that while he is the incarnation of Rasa-manjari, some say Raghunatha dasa Gosvami was the incarnation of Rati-manjari or Bhanumati-devi.

questions.question: Which of these is listed as a potential identity for Raghunatha dasa Gosvami in addition to Rasa-manjari?
questions.explanation: Verse 186 notes that some say Raghunatha dasa Gosvami was the incarnation of Rati-manjari.

187.verse_text: Bhugarbha Thakura was the incarnation of Prema-manjari. Lokanatha Gosvami was the incarnation of Lila-manjari.

questions.question: Which gopi is identified as the previous incarnation of Bhugarbha Thakura?
questions.explanation: Verse 187 explicitly states that Bhugarbha Thakura was the incarnation of Prema-manjari.

questions.question: Who is identified in the text as the incarnation of Lila-manjari?

188.verse_text: The vraja-gopis Kalavati-devi, Rasollasa-devi, and Gunatunga-devi, who sang the songs composed by Visakha-devi, later appeared as Govinda, Madhavananda, and Vasudeva respectively.

questions.question: Which personality appeared as Madhavananda in the pastimes of Lord Caitanya?

questions.question: In Lord Caitanya's pastimes, who did the vraja-gopi Gunatunga-devi manifest as?

questions.question: Who was the vraja-gopi that appeared as Govinda?
questions.explanation: The text states that the vraja-gopi Kalavati-devi appeared as Govinda.

questions.question: Whose songs were sung by Kalavati-devi, Rasollasa-devi, and Gunatunga-devi?
questions.explanation: The verse explicitly mentions that Kalavati-devi, Rasollasa-devi, and Gunatunga-devi sang the songs composed by Visakha-devi.

189.verse_text: Srimati Radharani's maidservants, Rangalekha-devi and Kalakeli-devi, appeared as Sikhi-mahiti and his sister, Madhavi-devi respectively.

questions.question: Which personality in the pastimes of Lord Caitanya is identified as the incarnation of the gopi Rangalekha-devi?
questions.explanation: According to verse 189, Srimati Radharani's maidservant Rangalekha-devi appeared as Sikhi-mahiti.

questions.question: Who is the eternal identity of Madhavi-devi within the context of Srimati Radharani's maidservants?
questions.explanation: Verse 189 states that Kalakeli-devi, one of Srimati Radharani's maidservants, appeared as Madhavi-devi.

questions.question: What is the relationship between Sikhi-mahiti and Madhavi-devi as described in Gaura Ganoddesha Dipika?
questions.explanation: The verse explicitly describes Madhavi-devi as the sister of Sikhi-mahiti.

190.verse_text: Malli-devi, the daughter of a Pulinda, appeared as Kalidasa.;

questions.question: In the divine pastimes of Lord Caitanya, who is identified as the incarnation of Malli-devi?

questions.question: What was the previous identity of the devotee Kalidasa?
questions.explanation: Verse 190 states that Malli-devi, who was the daughter of a Pulinda, appeared as Kalidasa.

191.verse_text: Suklambara Brahmacari was the incarnation of one of the yajna-patnis who gave food in charity to Krsna and his friends. Lord Caitanya was accustomed to eating food He had begged from Suklambara Brahmacari. questions.question: Which divine personality is Suklambara Brahmacari identified as in the pastimes of Lord Caitanya?
questions.explanation: The text states that Suklambara Brahmacari was the incarnation of one of the yajna-patnis who gave food in charity to Krsna and His friends.

questions.question: Which activity of Lord Caitanya specifically involved Suklambara Brahmacari?
options: They traveled together to South India
Lord Caitanya was accustomed to eating food begged from him
Suklambara Brahmacari served as Lord Caitanya's personal cook
questions.correct_answer: Lord Caitanya was accustomed to eating food begged from him
questions.explanation: The text explicitly mentions that Lord Caitanya was accustomed to eating food He had begged from Suklambara Brahmacari.

questions.question: Besides being identified as one of the yajna-patnis, who else do some say Suklambara Brahmacari was an incarnation of?
options: A sage from the Dandaka forest
questions.explanation: The text notes that while some identify him as a yajna-patni, others believe he was one of the husbands of the yajna-patnis, known as the yajnika-brahmanas.

192.verse_text:
Two other yajna-patnis appeared as Jagadisa and Hiranya. One ekadasi day Lord Caitanya ate grains

he had begged from them.

questions.question: Which two associates of Lord Caitanya are identified as being incarnations of two of the yajna-patnis?
questions.explanation: Verse 192 states that two other yajna-patnis appeared as Jagadisa and Hiranya.

questions.question: What significant action did Lord Caitanya perform on an Ekadasi day involving Jagadisa and Hiranya?
options: He ate grains begged from them
questions.correct_answer: He ate grains begged from them
questions.explanation: The verse explicitly states that on one ekadasi day, Lord Caitanya ate grains He had begged from Jagadisa and Hiranya.

193.verse_text: Kubja, who had been very to dear to Lord Krsna in Mathura, later appeared in Jagannatha Puri as Kasi Misra, who was very dear to Lord Caitanya.

questions.question: Which personality who lived in Jagannatha Puri is identified as the incarnation of Kubja?
questions.explanation: Verse 193 explicitly states that Kubja, who was dear to Lord Krsna in Mathura, appeared in Jagannatha Puri as Kasi Misra, who was dear to Lord Caitanya.

questions.question: Who was the author of the book Sri Krsna-prema-tarangini among Lord Caitanya's associates?
questions.explanation: The text identifies Sriman Bhagavatacarya as the individual who was very dear to Lord Caitanya and wrote the book Sri Krsna-prema-tarangini.

questions.question: Why did Lord Caitanya give the name 'Jitamitra' to one of His associates?
options: Because he was born in the Catta dynasty
Because he lived in the village of Campahatta
Because he had completely subdued lust and the other five enemies of the conditioned soul
Because he was an unalloyed servant who served at Jagannatha Puri
questions.correct_answer: Because he had completely subdued lust and the other five enemies of the conditioned soul
questions.explanation: The text explains that the name Jitamitra, meaning 'conqueror of the enemies', was given by Lord Caitanya because the associate had completely subdued lust and the five enemies of the conditioned soul.

questions.question: Which of these individuals was the author of Sri Krsna-stavavali?
questions.explanation: The text identifies Paramananda Gupta as the author of the book Sri Krsna-stavavali among the associates who were formerly maidservants of Srimati Radharani.

questions.question: The maidservants of Srimati Radharani appeared among Lord Caitanya's associates by assuming what kind of form?
questions.explanation: The text states that the listed maidservants of Srimati Radharani assumed male forms to appear among Lord Caitanya's associates.

194-207.verse_text:
Malati, Candralatika, Manjumedha, Varangada, Ratnavali, Kamala, Gunacuda, Sukesini, Karpura- manjari, Syama-manjari, Sveta-manjari, Vilasa-manjari, Kamalekha, Mauna-manjari, Gandhonmada, Rasonmada, Candrika, and Candrasekhara, were among the maidservants of Srimati Radharani.
Assuming male forms they appeared among Lord Caitanya's associates, and each displayed his particular kind of ecstatic love for the Lord. These gopis became Subhananda Dvija, Sridhara Brahmacari, Paramananda Gupta (who wrote the book Sri Krsna-stavavali), Raghunatha Dvija (who was an unalloyed servant of Lord Caitanya), Kamsari Sena, Sri Jagannatha Sena Mahasaya, Subuddhi Misra, Sriharsa, Raghu Misra (who was the best of the brahmanas), Jitamitra (whose appropriate name, meaning 'conqueror of the enemies', was given by Lord Caitanya because Jitamitra had completely subdued lust and the other five enemies of the conditioned soul), Sriman Bhagavatacarya (who was very dear to Lord Caitanya, and who wrote the book Sri Krsna-prema- tarangini), Sriman Jiva Pandita (who was the saintly son of Sri Vallabha), Vaninatha Dvija (who lived in the village of Campahatta, and who was very dear to Lord Caitanya), Isanacarya, Kamala, Laksminatha Pandita, Ganga-mantri, Jagannatha Mamu (who was a very elevated brahmana), Srikanthabharana Ananta (who was born in the Catta dynasty), Hastigopala (who lived in Rangaksetra, and who was very dear to Lord Caitanya), Hari Acarya, Srinayana Misra (who was a close associate of Lord Caitanya), Kavidatta, Ramadasa, Ciranjiva, and Sulocana respectively.

questions.question: Which personality identified as an associate of Lord Caitanya is noted for writing the book Sri Krsna-stavavali?
questions.explanation: The text explicitly states that Paramananda Gupta, who was a maidservant of Srimati Radharani named Ratnavali, wrote the book Sri Krsna-stavavali.

questions.question: Who was given the name 'Jitamitra' by Lord Caitanya because he had completely subdued lust and the other five enemies of the conditioned soul?
questions.explanation: The text explains that the name Jitamitra was given by Lord Caitanya, signifying 'conqueror of the enemies', because the devotee had subdued lust and the five enemies.

questions.question: Which of the following associates of Lord Caitanya is identified as the author of the book Sri Krsna-prema-tarangini?
questions.explanation: The text identifies Sriman Bhagavatacarya as being very dear to Lord Caitanya and the author of the book Sri Krsna-prema-tarangini.

questions.question: In the context of the associates of Lord Caitanya, which devotee resided in the village of Campahatta?
questions.explanation: The text specifies that Vaninatha Dvija lived in the village of Campahatta and was very dear to Lord Caitanya.

questions.question: Which of these associates was born in the Catta dynasty?
questions.explanation: The text identifies Srikanthabharana Ananta as an associate of Lord Caitanya who was born in the Catta dynasty.

208.verse_text: Someone may consider that among these devotees some are greatly exalted and others are a little less. I will not make any such distinctions. For me all these great souls are equal.

questions.question: What is the author's stance regarding the relative spiritual standing of the devotees described in his work?
options: He considers those who wrote books to be higher than those who did not.
He does not make distinctions, considering all these great souls to be equal.
He ranks them according to their historical service to Lord Caitanya.
He identifies the ones living in Srihatta as the most exalted.
questions.correct_answer: He does not make distinctions, considering all these great souls to be equal.
questions.explanation: Verse 208 explicitly states that the author refuses to make distinctions between the devotees, declaring that for him, all these great souls are equal.

questions.question: Which of the following attitudes does the author express regarding the devotees of Lord Caitanya?
options: A preference for those who lived in Srihatta.
A desire to categorize them by their level of ecstasy.
A refusal to distinguish between them in terms of their exalted status.
A belief that only the authors of devotional books are truly great.
questions.correct_answer: A refusal to distinguish between them in terms of their exalted status.
questions.explanation: The author clarifies in verse 208 that while some might think some devotees are more exalted than others, he himself does not make any such distinctions.

questions.question: According to the author's stated viewpoint, how should the different associates of Lord Caitanya be viewed?
options: As having varying levels of importance based on their service.
As all being equal great souls.
As being divided into those who were gopis and those who were not.
As being ranked solely by their proximity to the Lord.
questions.correct_answer: As all being equal great souls.
questions.explanation: Verse 208 explicitly rejects the notion of hierarchy among these devotees, stating that for the author, all of them are equal.

209.verse_text: Ciranjiva and Sulocana lived in the village of Srihatta. They were completely surrendered to Lord Caitanya, and because they were the friends and helpers of Narahari dasa they were honored and considered great souls.

questions.question: Which village was the home of Ciranjiva and Sulocana?
questions.explanation: Verse 209 explicitly identifies that Ciranjiva and Sulocana lived in the village of Srihatta.

questions.question: Ciranjiva and Sulocana were known as the friends and helpers of which associate of Lord Caitanya?
questions.explanation: Verse 209 states that Ciranjiva and Sulocana were considered great souls because they were the friends and helpers of Narahari dasa.

questions.question: Why were Ciranjiva and Sulocana considered great souls?
options: Because they were born in a holy land
Because they were friends and helpers of Narahari dasa
Because they were the spiritual masters of the author
Because they were residents of Navadvipa
questions.correct_answer: Because they were friends and helpers of Narahari dasa
questions.explanation: The verse states they were honored and considered great souls specifically because they were friends and helpers of Narahari dasa.

210.verse_text: The scriptures state: "A disciple should not address his spiritual master by name". For this reason I have not mentioned the previous name of my spiritual master, Sri Srinatha Cakravarti.

questions.question: Who is the spiritual master of Kavi Karnapura?
questions.explanation: Verse 210 explicitly identifies Sri Srinatha Cakravarti as the author's spiritual master.

questions.question: Why does Kavi Karnapura refrain from mentioning the previous name of his spiritual master?
options: The name was forgotten by the author.
It is forbidden by the scriptures for a disciple to address a spiritual master by name.
The spiritual master requested that his name be kept secret.
The name was considered inauspicious by local tradition.
questions.correct_answer: It is forbidden by the scriptures for a disciple to address a spiritual master by name.
questions.explanation: Verse 210 states that the author does not mention his spiritual master's previous name because scripture dictates that a disciple should not address their spiritual master by name.

211.verse_text: All glories to Sri Krsnadeva of Kumara-hatta, the author of the commentary on Sri Bhagavata- samhita. His fame is always splendidly manifest.

questions.question: Which specific location is associated with the residence of Sri Krsnadeva?
questions.explanation: The verse explicitly states that Sri Krsnadeva is of Kumara-hatta.

questions.question: What is the noted literary contribution of Sri Krsnadeva of Kumara-hatta?
questions.explanation: The text identifies Sri Krsnadeva as the author of the commentary on Sri Bhagavata-samhita.

212.verse_text:
If I have made any errors in describing the great souls mentioned in this book, I beg those great
personalities to please forgive me. Neither Lord Brahma, Lord Siva, nor Lord Ananta Sesa are able

to describe completely the exalted virtues of those great souls.

questions.question: Which divine beings are explicitly cited as being unable to completely describe the exalted virtues of the great souls mentioned in Gaura Ganoddesha Dipika?
options: Lord Brahma, Lord Siva, and Lord Ananta Sesa
Lord Indra, Lord Vayu, and Lord Varuna
Lord Brahma, Lord Visnu, and Lord Mahesvara
Lord Caitanya, Lord Nityananda, and Advaita Acarya
questions.correct_answer: Lord Brahma, Lord Siva, and Lord Ananta Sesa
questions.explanation: The text states that Lord Brahma, Lord Siva, and Lord Ananta Sesa are not able to completely describe the exalted virtues of the great souls featured in the work.

questions.question: What is the author's request concerning the descriptions of the great souls presented in the book?
options: He asks that they be verified by the Mimamsakas.
He asks the great personalities to forgive any errors made in the description.
He asks that the book be translated into many languages.
He asks the readers to memorize the identities listed.
questions.correct_answer: He asks the great personalities to forgive any errors made in the description.
questions.explanation: The author explicitly asks the great souls mentioned in the book to forgive him if he has made any errors in describing them.

213.verse_text: I have written this book in such a way that the Mimamsakas, false logicians, and others who relish dry philosophical debate will not be able to understand it, even with the greatest endeavor. Only the devotees, who are learned in the rasa-sastras, and who continually take shelter of Lord Caitanya's lotus feet, will be able to understand this book properly. I have written this book for them.

questions.question: Which specific group of individuals will be unable to understand the contents of the Gaura-ganoddesa-dipika, despite their greatest efforts?
questions.explanation: Verse 213 explicitly states that Mimamsakas, false logicians, and those who relish dry philosophical debate will not be able to understand the book.

questions.question: Who are the only individuals capable of properly understanding the Gaura-ganoddesa-dipika?
options: Those who have mastered the art of philosophical debate
The devotees who are learned in rasa-sastras and take shelter of Lord Caitanya's lotus feet
The scholars who have studied all the Vedic literatures
Those who possess great material intelligence
questions.correct_answer: The devotees who are learned in rasa-sastras and take shelter of Lord Caitanya's lotus feet
questions.explanation: The verse specifies that the work is intended for and understood only by devotees who are learned in rasa-sastras and continually take shelter of Lord Caitanya's lotus feet.

questions.question: Why did the author write the Gaura-ganoddesa-dipika in a manner that excludes dry philosophical thinkers from understanding it?
options: Because it is written specifically for devotees who relish the rasa-sastras
Because the author wanted to prevent arguments
Because the author did not have access to logic
Because the book only contains simple stories
questions.correct_answer: Because it is written specifically for devotees who relish the rasa-sastras
questions.explanation: The text states that the author wrote the book specifically for those who are learned in the rasa-sastras and take shelter of Lord Caitanya.

214.verse_text: I pray that this book I have written, the Sri Gaura-ganoddesa-dipika (a lamp to see the associates of Lord Caitanya), may shine in the homes of the blissful devotees of the Lord.

questions.question: What is the metaphorical title given to the book Sri Gaura-ganoddesa-dipika in the main verse?
options: A lamp to see the associates of Lord Caitanya
A map of the spiritual world
questions.correct_answer: A lamp to see the associates of Lord Caitanya
questions.explanation: Verse 214 explicitly defines the title 'Sri Gaura-ganoddesa-dipika' as 'a lamp to see the associates of Lord Caitanya'.

questions.question: In the homes of which group of people does Kavi Karnapura pray that his book will shine?
options: The great scholars of the Mimamsa school
The blissful devotees of the Lord
questions.correct_answer: The blissful devotees of the Lord
questions.explanation: Verse 214 states, 'I pray that this book I have written... may shine in the homes of the blissful devotees of the Lord.'

215.verse_text: This book was written in the year 1498 of the Saka era (corresponding to 1576 A.D.). I pray that those great devotees whose minds are immersed in the nectar of Lord Caitanyacandra's pastimes may read this book critically and correct all its faults.

questions.question: In what year of the Saka era was the Sri Gaura-ganoddesa-dipika written?
questions.explanation: The text explicitly states that the book was written in the year 1498 of the Saka era.

questions.explanation: The text explicitly links the Saka era year 1498 to the year 1576 A.D.
